acceptance_criteria.text: Overflow from the session panel area is contained inside the panel block instead of making the overall page/workspace shell scroll. The page layout remains height-constrained, while the panel rail and panel contents use internal scrolling where needed.
proof: src/App.tsx:1494 keeps the workspace section overflow-hidden.
src/App.tsx:1601 adds data-testid="session-panel-scroll-region" with overflow-auto overscroll-contain.
e2e/usage-recordings.spec.ts:108 asserts the panel scroll region uses overflow-x auto.
Command artifacts PASS: .agent/tasks/compact-tree-layout/raw/test-unit.txt, .agent/tasks/compact-tree-layout/raw/build.txt, .agent/tasks/compact-tree-layout/raw/test-integration.txt.
gaps: Top-level page scroll is inferred from layout code and targeted scroll-region coverage rather than a direct browser scroll-position assertion.

acceptance_criteria.text: Panel headers stop showing full workspace or folder paths by default. Files panels show a concise project-relative location label, and other panel headers use short labels instead of repeating the full absolute workdir path.
proof: src/App.tsx:140 adds toRelativePathLabel(...) for concise relative path labels.
src/App.tsx:1000, src/App.tsx:1034, and src/App.tsx:1057 use relative labels in Files and Editor surfaces.
src/App.tsx:1549 shortens the session runtime workspace label to a relative workspace label.
e2e/usage-recordings.spec.ts:232 and e2e/usage-recordings.spec.ts:241 verify focused editor paths render as README.md and src/nested/index.ts.
gaps: Files panel subtitle is verified mainly by code inspection.

acceptance_criteria.text: Editor panels use the full available panel height for the editor surface, and the editor area scrolls internally without collapsing into a short viewport.
proof: src/components/editor-panel.tsx:57 and src/components/editor-panel.tsx:58 make the editor surface flex to full height.
src/styles.css:57 and src/styles.css:69 ensure the CodeMirror theme/scroller fills height and scrolls internally.
Command artifacts PASS: .agent/tasks/compact-tree-layout/raw/build.txt and .agent/tasks/compact-tree-layout/raw/test-integration.txt.
gaps: Full-height editor behavior is primarily code-inspected.

acceptance_criteria.text: The top-left sidebar header shows only the product title `The Big IDE` and the new-project entry point. It does not show the active project name, filesystem path, or runtime-mode/path text.
proof: src/App.tsx:1362 renders the static workspace-app-title heading as The Big IDE.
e2e/usage-recordings.spec.ts:15 asserts the header title text is exactly The Big IDE.
The old active project/path sidebar header block was removed from src/App.tsx.

acceptance_criteria.text: The project/session sidebar becomes more compact and tree-like: projects render as lightweight rows or branches with nested session rows, reduced card chrome, and clearer hierarchy while preserving selection, session creation, and session lifecycle actions.
proof: src/App.tsx:194 introduces expandedProjects state for collapsible tree branches.
src/App.tsx:1389 and src/App.tsx:1450 render lightweight project rows with nested session rows instead of bordered cards.
Project and session interaction hooks remain on the compact tree rows.
gaps: The visual compactness/tree styling is confirmed by code inspection rather than screenshot comparison.

acceptance_criteria.text: The compact sidebar layout keeps the existing project/session flows usable: creating a project still uses the existing modal flow, creating a session still happens from the relevant project item, and active project/session selection remains clear.
proof: src/App.tsx:1366 preserves new-project-button in the compact header.
src/App.tsx:1430 preserves new-session-button on each project row.
src/App.tsx:1450 preserves session-row selection within the nested tree.
e2e/usage-recordings.spec.ts:17, e2e/usage-recordings.spec.ts:25, and e2e/usage-recordings.spec.ts:129 cover project creation, session creation, and session lifecycle/selection.

acceptance_criteria.text: Existing file-opening behavior remains intact after the compact layout changes: opening files from Files still focuses an already-open editor panel for that file, otherwise opens one editor panel per file.
proof: src/App.tsx:687, src/App.tsx:696, src/App.tsx:704, and src/App.tsx:718 preserve the focus-or-open editor logic.
e2e/usage-recordings.spec.ts:230 and e2e/usage-recordings.spec.ts:235 verify duplicate opens do not create extra editor panels.
e2e/usage-recordings.spec.ts:240 verifies a second file creates a second editor panel.

known_gaps: AC1, AC2, AC3, and AC5 rely partly on code inspection for layout/visual proof.
Lint was not run for this task; raw/lint.txt records this explicitly.
